citations.desc: Environment enhancement to promote psychological well-being.
citations.narrative:
There is no developed and documented plan for environment enhancement for screech owl nestlings, chicks or fledglings.
At time of inspection, a single 3-week-old juvenile screech owl is being hand-raised, after separation from its parents at
one week of age. Upon speaking to the attending veterinarian, they were not aware that the chick was being hand raised
and therefore had not been given the opportunity to discuss recommendations with the facility. Failure to develop and
document a plan for chicks, nestlings, or fledglings, considered special consideration birds, may result in inadequate care
in promoting and addressing their psychological well-being. The facility must develop, document, and provide special
attention to this screech owl chick regarding enhancement of their environment, based on the needs of the individual
species and/or individual bird, to promote psychological well-being in accordance with the instructions of their attending
veterinarian.
Correct by: 05/15/2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
Additional Inspectors:
KRISTIN NAPOLI, SUPERVISORY ANIMAL CARE SPECIALIST n
